items.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz open as Lebanon ceasefire begins

children.content: US President Donald Trump immediately welcomed the news from Tehran but went on to warn that the US blockade of Iran's ports would continue.

articles.title: Oil prices plunge after news Strait of Hormuz to open

children.content: Oil prices plunged more than 10 per cent after Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz would be "completely open" but shipping giants are approaching the situation with caution.

image.altText: Oil tanker off US coast

gallery.altText: A large tanker sits on the ocean against a mountainous landscape.

thumbnail.altText: A large tanker sits on the ocean against a mountainous landscape.

items.title: Trump says US will go into Iran to remove 'nuclear dust'

children.content: Should a peace deal with Tehran be reached, the US president says he expects the US to work with Iran to recover what's left of its enriched uranium. Follow live.

image.altText: An elderly man with a yellow-ish hue and bright pink tie makes a strange gesture.

items.title: The world faces a grim reality, even if Trump exists in a different one

children.content: The world is stuck watching a performative series of announcements about meetings that could, may, will happen, involving a president not dealing with the reality that he is in a weak and confused bargaining position.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands outside the White House as reporters hold microphones.

items.title: Budget coincides with the thunderclap of history in flux — and hard choices

children.content: The budget's usual timing on the second Tuesday of May is an artifice of political convention. This year's edition just happens to be coinciding with the thunderclap of history in flux.

image.altText: A close up of Albanese, who wears a neutral expression

items.title: What crisis? Why stocks are ignoring the economy

children.content: The Australian Securities Exchange is within striking distance of new records as economists issue worst-case warnings of a close call for a global recession.

image.altText: Pedestrians are reflected in a window with an indicator board displaying stock prices at the Australian Stock Exchange in Sydney

items.title: As Trump promotes a new ceasefire, many in Lebanon fear a bad deal

children.content: Donald Trump sold a ceasefire in Lebanon as a step towards peace. But that promise hinges on a condition that Lebanon may not be able to deliver.

A displaced man gestures lying over belongings on a mini pickup, in Qasmiyeh Lebanon as he returns his family to their village

items.title: Outcry over 'disturbing' video of off-duty police officer pinning 13yo girl on train

children.content: Video of an off-duty police officer restraining a 13yo girl on a Perth train has sparked accusations of excessive force.

image.altText: A young girl holds her head and winces with her face being blurred.

items.title: Australia enters drone warfare era with Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark

children.content: Australia is on a push to keep pace with the rapid evolution of drone warfare, with an emphasis on locally produced Ghost Bat and Ghost Shark systems.

image.altText: A black mini submarine looking drone seen underwater.

items.title: Labor considering ways to spare new homes from capital gains changes

children.content: The possible budget centrepiece is likely to be finalised much closer to the May deadline than usual this year given global uncertainty, but progress has been made in recent weeks.

image.altText: A man in suit and tie holds his hands up in front of him as he speaks seriously.

items.title: Turbulence ahead as airfares fluctuate amid Middle East conflict

children.content: Australians are finding domestic flights for as little as $65 on some routes, while others are paying thousands to travel interstate as airlines quietly reshape the market in response to conflict in the Middle East.

image.altText: The undercarriage of a jet plane as it takes off.

items.title: The Windsor playbook with a twist: Harry and Meghan's royal model

children.content: Mixing philanthropy, influencing and brand building, Harry and Meghan’s Australian tour revives a question Queen Elizabeth once shut down: can the 'half in, half out' royal life work?

items.title: Aung San Suu Kyi to be freed sooner as Myanmar releases her ally from jail

children.content: Myanmar's ex-leader Aung San Suu Kyi has her prison sentence cut as thousands of prisoners are freed. Meanwhile, the UN reveals 2025 marked the deadliest year on record for Rohingya refugees fleeing Myanmar.

image.altText: Aung San Suu Ky looks intensely into the camera at a conference.

items.title: Daughter tells jury she saw mum crush drugs into lethal smoothie

children.content: The daughter of a Gold Coast woman accused of killing her son alleges she saw her mum use a hammer to crush up drugs before putting them into a lethal fruit smoothie.

image.altText: A woman with sunglasses and wearing a pink and white shirt.

items.title: Chinese device found in strategic waters near Bali identified

children.content: A large torpedo-shaped device discovered near an island in a strategically important strait between Bali and Lombok has been identified by defence analysts as a Chinese undersea monitoring system.

gallery.altText: A torpedo-like object in the shallows at a beach.

thumbnail.altText: A torpedo-like object in the shallows at a beach.

items.title: Popular outback motoring rally cancelled due to fuel crisis

children.content: As fuel becomes less available, Variety NSW is concerned its main fundraising event could harm the small communities it tries to support.

image.altText: A car decorated like Thomas the Tank Engine driving through the outback

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of the Big Day Out, footy fairytales and the circles of hell

children.content: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

image.altText: The three members of Nirvana pose for a photo.

items.title: It's generational warfare as millennials take on gen Z in Beef season 2

children.content: The dark comedy series returns with a biting look at generational conflict, starring Carey Mulligan and Oscar Isaac as a "combustible" middle-aged couple.

children.content: Our critics' picks for your weekend entertainment from the small screen to the kitchen oven.

items.title: 'Derek' the dingo a concern for some as he roams streets in outback town

children.content: An lone dingo, affectionately known as Derek, has been spotted in suburban streets, backyards and even in dog parks, raising some concern for Alice Springs residents.

gallery.altText: A dingo walking on a street

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith leaves jail after securing bail over alleged war crimes

children.content: Ben Roberts-Smith was granted bail after spending a week in custody over alleged war crime of murder offences.

image.altText: an aerial vision of a car taking Ben Roberts-Smith out of silverwater jail

items.title: Young people leaving Sydney 'en masse' over housing affordability

children.content: Productivity Commissioner Danielle Wood says Australia will not meet the federal government's 1.2 million new home target, as residential construction rates fall.

image.altText: People walk through the front door of a house, past a For Lease sign

items.title: 'Completely broken': Parents share anguish after fatal police collision

children.content: The parents of 16-year-old Jai Kalani Wright, who died after colliding with a police car, share their emotional testimonies in court as the sentencing hearing begins.

image.altText: Jai's dad is wearing a bright coloured jersey and his mother is hugging another woman

items.title: Panthers win golden point thriller but lose key man Kenny

children.content: Penrith have paid a heavy price for their extra time win over the Dolphins with hooker Mitch Kenny facing a lengthy stint on the sidelines.

image.altText: Mitch Kenny grimaces after an injury

items.title: AI Val Kilmer branded 'terrifying', but filmmakers say movie is ethical

children.content: A trailer for a film with an AI-generated character in the likeness of the late Val Kilmer has been slammed by social media users, but the filmmakers are defending their work.

thumbnail.altText: An AI-generated image of Val Kilmer with his hands on the shoulders of a girl with pigtails

items.title: Bulldogs cop injury carnage on 'traumatic' night with ACL fears for superstar Darcy

children.content: The Bulldogs are trounced by 75 points and lose Sam Darcy to a knee injury as Sydney retains its supremacy against GWS.

image.altText: Sam Darcy clutches his knee while laying face down on the turf

items.title: Europe was a word British PMs dared not say. Then Donald Trump returned

children.content: Keir Starmer was never a fan of Brexit. Now, under the cover of the war in Iran and a fractured US relationship, he's looking to Europe to return Britain to the fold.

image.altText: Keir Starmer speaks in the House of Commons

avatar.alt: Brett Worthington stands in a courtyard inside parliament house

items.title: PM joins leaders meeting on securing Strait of Hormuz

children.content: Anthony Albanese has joined a virtual meeting of world leaders to discuss how the Strait of Hormuz can be secured after the conflict in the Middle East ends.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese

items.title: Australian woman killed in alleged DV murder in the US

children.content: A woman from the NSW Central Coast has died from stab wounds allegedly inflicted by her husband at their home in the US state of Florida.

image.altText: A woman with short blonde hair wearing a black hat, she is smiling.

items.title: Travellers in Europe may face major flight disruption over jet fuel crisis

children.content: Europe could experience major flight disruptions within weeks as global energy officials warn the continent has "maybe six weeks of jet fuel left", which could lead to further price hikes and cancellations for Australian travellers.

image.altText: A generic under carriage of a plane taking off into the sky.

items.title: Pacific leaders declare emergency over potential fuel shortages

children.content: The prime minister of Solomon Islands says Pacific leaders have declared an emergency over the looming threat of fuel shortages, while the Australian government contemplates a plan which could see stocks of diesel held by the US military sent to Pacific nations suffering shortages.

image.altText: A petrol station at night with vehicles lining up to fill up.

items.title: 100 bombs in 10 minutes: Mapping Lebanon's 'Black Wednesday'

children.content: The Israel Defense Forces labelled it Operation Eternal Darkness. Inside Lebanon, it's now called Black Wednesday. ABC NEWS Verify has geolocated the explosions across 10 minutes of chaos.

gallery.altText: A composite image featuring the aftermath of April 8 strikes on Lebanon with a 10-minute clock super-imposed.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image featuring the aftermath of April 8 strikes on Lebanon with a 10-minute clock super-imposed.

items.title: Government advisory group urges delay of NDIS 'robo' changes

children.content: The federal government has been urged by its own NDIS advisory group to delay the rollout of a controversial new way of determining support plans for participants.

image.altText: An NDIS logo is seen on a building

items.title: How mobile phone cameras have helped unearth a mysterious kingdom

children.content: An exhibition bridges the gap between art and science by inviting visitors to imagine the world through the senses of other species and consider our own fragile relationship with nature.

image.altText: A cluster of cup-shaped mushrooms cling to a damp fallen branch amonst feathery ferns.

items.title: What we know about the 'small fire' that became an 'unprecedented' Geelong refinery blaze

children.content: The "unprecedented" fire at one of the two remaining oil refineries in Australia took more than 12 hours to extinguish. Here's what we know about how it unfolded.

articles.title: Queensland government in discussions for new oil refinery

children.content: As the fuel crisis continues, a proposal for an $11 billion new oil refinery in central Queensland has been put forward.

image.altText: A close up of the exhaust pipes at a petrol refinery.

items.title: Oil refinery fire will not push Australia to fuel plan 'stage three', PM says

children.content: Anthony Albanese has cut a trip to Malaysia short to visit Viva Energy Refinery in Geelong, which went up in flames late on Wednesday.

image.altText: A close up of Albanese, who wears a neutral expression

items.title: 'Never thought we'd be homeless': Mother of 13 pleads for help

children.content: Finding a home for a large family in a regional area has left one family camping on the side of the highway in rural New South Wales. Nationally, Indigenous people are over-represented in homelessness statistics.

image.altText: A boy eats his noodles in a camp chair.
